news.title: In the wake of Graham Platner’s success (and fall), a candidate to replace him changes his mind about ‘genocide’
news.description: (JTA) — When Jordan Wood vied last fall for the Democratic nomination for Maine’s U.S. Senate seat, he avoided accusing Israel of genocide, citing a link between rising antisemitism and “the language” that people use. Graham Platner, who went on to overwhelmingly win the nomination, did not stint on using the term. Platner is out... A lot of people commenting on the maine Senate race fail to understand that Nirav Shah got the most first round votes of any one in the governors race, including Pingree. There are a hell of a lot of people who voted for both Platner and Shah as first round picks.

BREAKING: New polling from Survey USA shows Democrat Nirav Shah in the lead against Susan Collins in Maine. Polling also shows Shah is the best performing Democrat of any candidate now in the race against Collins. @democraticwins.bsky.social 
@cnn

Nirav Shah and Troy Jackson solidify status as top contenders in Maine as others Senate candidates flounder

https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/n...

Thanks! With such a short timeline, we can't have another candidate who isn't thoroughly vetted. I've never heard of him, but on paper he sounds like he needs to pick a lane:

Nirav Dinesh Shah... epidemiologist, economist, attorney, and politician. He grew up in WI & KY, then was in IL, now in ME..
Nirav Shah impressive in Maine senate debate
Nirav Shah impressive in Maine senate debate
www.huffpost.com/entry/progre...

Nobody was particularly different in policy overall. They all hit the highlights of the progressive movement, mostly.

Nirav Shah, Dan Kleban and David Costello stand out as well polished speakers.  They seem to handle public pressure well and were well rehearsed.

3/

Maine Democratic Senate debate.  Personal opinion and observations.

Part 1: Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah and Jordan Wood 
youtu.be/3MuOr6Nnki0?...

Part 2: David Costello, Elizabeth Dickerson, Dan Kleban and Ashley Webb 
youtu.be/r5UQ9pzAr-o?...

1/

Overall in the Maine Senate race, the four candidates from the first debate would all be okay, though all have issues. Nirav Shah and Jordan Wood significantly improved their standing with me in this debate. Shenna Bellows hurt her standing. Troy Jackson failed to impress, but his platform is good.
Nirav Shah is looking like a very good replacement in Maine.
Arguably politics shouldn’t be a personality contest but seeing how it is, I think Maine’s best chance to defeat Collins is with either Sheena Bellows, Nirav Shah, or Dan Kleban.

Okay, final thoughts on the first-half debate:

Nirav Shah took this away. Clear winner of the debate! His funding remains suspect, but he's giving the best answers by far.

Shenna Bellows completely turned me off from her with her answers.

Nirav Shah: I've got three letters for you: RFK. One of Collin's most odious Senate confirmation votes. We've had zero oversight with Collins. I'd seek a committee assignment on the health committee to hold Kennedy to account.
“My North Star is Medicare for all because health care is a human right.” - Nirav Shah
Also Nirav Shah: My north star is Medicare for All, but we can also work on other reforms along the way there.
Nirav Shah: I'll work with Republicans to make advances on our nation's healthcare. Neither side thinks insurance companies are working for us.
Nirav Shah: I'm not an apologist for Maduro, but Trump did not have the authority to abduct him. Finally a clear answer.
Nirav Shah: Continue funding Ukraine as our ally. Reign in Putin. Don't embolden him with concessions.
Nirav Shah: Stop funding the genocide in Gaza.
Nirav Shah: We have a solemn obligation to maintain our pact with Americans. Millionaires need to pay their fair share. Scrap the cap.

Nirav Shah: Dispelling the idea that there just isn't enough money. We supposedly can't find money for hospitals, healthcare, etc., but we can always spend money on rogue agencies like ICE. "Not enough money" is an excuse. We have enough to spend on things that matter.
Gotta say so far Nirav Shah is impressive

Term limits on Supreme Court justices: Jordan Wood says no, doesn't trust Republicans. Shenna Bellows says "hell yes." Troy Jackson: yes for not only judges, but also Congress members. Nirav Shah: yes, applied across the spectrum.
Nirav Shah: codify the right to abortion into federal law.
Has anybody ever seen @chrislhayes.bsky.social and Nirav Shah in the same room?
Nirav Shah the only of these four candidates staying strong on "abolish ICE," not just reform it.
So far Nirav Shah is easiest on the ears. #MEPolitics
(L to R): Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah, Jordan Wood
Nirav Shah is the first to specify not just reforming, but abolishing ICE.

Neither Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, nor Nirav Shah decided to mention Palestine, trans rights, Medicare for All, or a wealth tax in their opening statements. Disappointing. Both relied on vague anti-Trump sentiments rather than getting into any specifics that sets them apart.

If they pick Shah, @FoxNews will play this “The primary catalyst for Nirav Shah’s departure was the intense backlash he faced regarding his handling of a recurring, deadly Legionnaires' disease outbreak at the Illinois Veterans Home in Quincy, which ultimately killed at least 13 veterans. “

1. Troy Jackson
2. Shenna Bellows
3. Jordan Wood
4. David Costello
5. Nirav Shah
6. Dan Kleban

.
MAINE DEMOCRATIC U.S. SENATE DEBATE
On News Center Maine and app, 
Thursday, July 16, 7:00PM

1st hr: Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah, & Jordan Wood

2nd hr: David Costello, Elizabeth Dickerson, Dan Kleban, & Ashley Webb.

LIVE debate recordings will be available afterward.

#MEpolitics

Not a bother at all.

Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah, and Jordan Wood, who all got 20% or more in their respective primaries in June.

Vote the bitch out...then tar and feather

Shenna Bellows and Troy Jackson, while neither is a polished speaker, both have earned respect among the working class.  Both at times were a little hesitant, but very passionate and determined.  

10/

First let me preface by saying I have been supporting Troy Jackson for Platner's replacement since the beginning.  I have also been supporting Shenna Bellows as an alternative.

That said, my observations...

2/

Maine Democratic Senate debate.  Personal opinion and observations.

Part 1: Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah and Jordan Wood 
youtu.be/3MuOr6Nnki0?...

Part 2: David Costello, Elizabeth Dickerson, Dan Kleban and Ashley Webb 
youtu.be/r5UQ9pzAr-o?...

1/

Overall in the Maine Senate race, the four candidates from the first debate would all be okay, though all have issues. Nirav Shah and Jordan Wood significantly improved their standing with me in this debate. Shenna Bellows hurt her standing. Troy Jackson failed to impress, but his platform is good.

Okay, final thoughts on the first-half debate:

Nirav Shah took this away. Clear winner of the debate! His funding remains suspect, but he's giving the best answers by far.

Shenna Bellows completely turned me off from her with her answers.

End daylight savings time: Shenna Bellows: There are reasons for and against, but won't commit to anything. Troy Jackson: I would not support it. Nirah Shah: Yes, but make sure it's an extra hour in the evening. Jordan Wood: Also a yes.
“[Platner] energized a movement that was already there,” Maine Secretary of State Shenna Bellows said.
Honestly, Shenna Bellows answers here are completely tanking any appreciation I had for her. Platitudes at best. Incoherent at worst.

Shenna Bellows: I have a lot of ideas. The path to regaining the US Senate runs through me. Without me, we won't be able to hold Trump accountable. (Apparently she thinks she's the only one that can win?)
Shenna Bellows: Democratic/Republican is a false binary. Seriously, what? Where has she been living this past decade?
Shenna Bellows on Venezuela: We can't trust Trump. No answer to whether abducting Maduro was right. "It's sometimes confusing."
Shenna Bellows: I will always support Ukraine in this moment. It's about defending democracy and standing up for freedom and against dictators and oppressors.
Shenna Bellows: End the war in Iran right now.

Watching Maine Dems debate for Maine Senate / love the plain speak from our candidates about the essentialness of abolishing ICE.  

I’m team Shenna!  www.bellowsformaine.com
Shenna Bellows: The story that there's not enough money resonates with people. Scrap the cap.
Shenna bellows: stop spending money on endless wars, and start spending on things that matter to people. We can and should have universal childcare for everyone in the country.

Term limits on Supreme Court justices: Jordan Wood says no, doesn't trust Republicans. Shenna Bellows says "hell yes." Troy Jackson: yes for not only judges, but also Congress members. Nirav Shah: yes, applied across the spectrum.
Shenna Bellows: expand the Supreme Court, term limits.
Shenna Bellows is "really struggling this week" on how to respond to ICE murdering innocent people. No mention of abolishing ICE or even specific reforms.
Shenna Bellows is also calling Israel's actions a genocide and committing to Medicare for All.
(L to R): Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah, Jordan Wood

Neither Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, nor Nirav Shah decided to mention Palestine, trans rights, Medicare for All, or a wealth tax in their opening statements. Disappointing. Both relied on vague anti-Trump sentiments rather than getting into any specifics that sets them apart.

1. Troy Jackson
2. Shenna Bellows
3. Jordan Wood
4. David Costello
5. Nirav Shah
6. Dan Kleban
Democrats need Shenna Bellows for Maine

.

As a Maine voter, I'd be perfectly happy with Troy Jackson as the nominee. He's been in the (Maine) public eye long enough that skeletons are unlikely to emerge from his closet.
